data.title: Novita AI Ranked as the Best Performing & Reliable Inference Layer
data.description: Novita AI has launched an API that offers over 120 LLMs, with models available on the day of release. The API is compatible with OpenAI and Anthropic and has been verified for high performance by Artificial Analysis. This initiative comes in response to the increasing need for open-source AI infrastructure. The announcement was made in San Francisco on April 21, 2026.

data.title: Sunlighten Introduces PulseIQ™: The Intelligent Platform Redefining Infrared Wellness
data.description: Sunlighten, a renowned innovator in infrared saunas, has introduced PulseIQ™, a technology capable of providing four unique wavelengths separately to cater to recovery, relaxation, and performance needs in each session. The launch took place in Overland Park, Kansas, as of April 21, 2026, as confirmed by the press release from the company.

data.title: CDC Foundation Announces Appointment of Mary Currier and Mark Volker to Board of Directors
data.description: In a press release dated April 21, 2026, the CDC Foundation revealed the addition of Mary Currier, MD, MPH, the ex-Mississippi state health officer, and Mark Volker, a former PricewaterhouseCoopers (PwC) partner, to its board of directors. These new appointments are expected to enhance the board with their extensive leadership experience.

data.title: 2026 Annual Meeting of the American College of Clinical Pharmacology®
data.description: The 2026 Annual Meeting of the American College of Clinical Pharmacology® held in ASHBURN, Va. on April 21, 2026, focused on leveraging evolving clinical pharmacology paradigms to enhance patient outcomes. The event saw participation from a significant number of industry experts and professionals. Key discussions revolved around the strategies to harness these changes effectively. The American College of Clinical Pharmacology® expressed satisfaction with the outcomes of the meeting, emphasizing the importance of adapting to new paradigms in the field.

data.title: Persistent Reports $1,654.4M FY26 Revenue with 17.4% Y-o-Y Growth and 15.6% EBIT Margin
data.description: Persistent Systems, based in Santa Clara, California and Pune, India, reported a revenue of $436.0 million in the fourth quarter, showing a 3.2% increase quarter-on-quarter. The company also declared a full-year dividend of ₹40 per share. This announcement was made during the release of their audited financial results for the quarter.

data.title: CORRECTING and REPLACING: CPI Announces Partnership with Street Charity® to Launch a Groundbreaking New Payments Solution to Combat Hunger
data.description: In Denver, a partnership between CPI and Street Charity has led to the introduction of paper prepaid meal cards aimed at addressing hunger issues in a society moving towards cashless transactions. These innovative meal cards serve as a tool in the fight against hunger by providing individuals in need with access to essential meals. This collaboration marks a significant step in combating hunger, particularly in a world where cash is becoming less prevalent.

data.title: Actress/Director Aisha Tyler’s Cocktail Company, losophē, Expands Its Super-Premium Cocktail Line with The New Mojito, Launching April 21
data.description: In Los Angeles, losophē, a super-premium organic ready-to-drink cocktail company co-founded by actress/director Aisha Tyler and spirits industry veteran James Kim, has introduced its second product, The New Mojito, released recently. This launch follows the popularity of their first release, The New Margarita, which debuted in spring 2024. The New Mojito is produced by losophē at a small-batch distillery in Sonoma County, California.

data.title: Resale on the Rise: Klarna's Resell Feature Grows 75% as Consumers Put Real Money Back in Their Pockets
data.description: In Stockholm, recent data from Klarna, a global digital bank and flexible payments provider, shows a surge in popularity of its in-app resell feature. The number of listings created through the app has seen a remarkable growth of up to 75% in the last 13 months. This trend indicates a shift in consumer behavior towards viewing their belongings as assets to earn money from, rather than as fixed expenses. This emphasizes a larger trend where consumers are embracing resale as a method to generate income from existing possessions.
